name: Chicken Biriani
recipeIngredient: 2 Inch piece Gingerroot; fresh - 2 cups Brown Basmati Rice (or Ordinary Brown Rice)
4 tablespoons Natural yogurt Salt to taste
Fresh ground pepper Fresh coriander leaves for garnish
recipeInstructions:
1. Thinly slice 1 onion and set it aside.
2. Puree the other chopped onions, garlic, gingerroot, curry powder, turmeric, and natural yogurt together until smooth. If you don't have a blender or food processor, grate the onions and gingerroot and mix with the remaining ingredients.
3. Add plenty of seasoning to taste.
4. Spoon this mixture evenly over the chicken thighs, ensuring all sides are coated.
5. Cover and refrigerate for 1-24 hours (the longer it sets, the better).
6. Heat oil in a large heavy-bottomed saucepan or flameproof casserole.
7. Add sliced onions, bay leaves, and cardamoms; cook, stirring often, until browned about 20 minutes.
8. Remove half the onions with a slotted spoon and set aside for garnish.
9. Push the remaining onions to one side of the pan.
10. Brown chicken pieces all over using the reserved marinage juices.
11. Add rice to the pan, scattering it between the chicken pieces.
12. Tuck the cinnamon stick in among the chicken.
13. Pour in 3 3/4 cups water and return the reserved onions.
14. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat and cover tightly.
15. Simmer for 30-35 minutes until rice is cooked and chicken is tender. The biriani should be moist.
16. Transfer to serving dish or serve directly from pan, sprinkling with reserved onion before eating.
17. Garnish with lemon wedges just before serving (squeeze juice over the birani).
